steps.title: Decode the variable and what the function represents
steps.content:
The variable $x$ represents the number of years after the end of 1953.

Since $x = 5$, we're looking at 5 years after the end of 1953, which is the end of 1958.

The function $f(x) = 242(1.040)^x$ models the VALUE of the bank account in dollars.

Therefore, when we evaluate $f(5) \approx 294$, we're finding the account value (in dollars) at $x = 5$, which is the end of 1958.

steps.title: Match the interpretation to the correct choice
steps.content:
Now we interpret what $f(5) \approx 294$ means: the account value is approximately \$294 at the end of 1958.
  • Choice A: 5 dollars greater — but $f(5)$ gives the total value, not a difference. Incorrect.
  • Choice B: 294 dollars at the end of 1958 — this is exactly what $f(5) \approx 294$ tells us.
  • Choice C: 5 times greater — misreads $x = 5$ as a multiplier rather than a time unit. Incorrect.
  • Choice D: increase by 294 dollars every 5 years — confuses the total value with a rate of increase. Incorrect.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
